questions.question: What is trigonometry?
questions.correct_answer: The study of relationships between angles and sides of a right-angled triangle.

questions.question: In which triangle are trigonometric ratios defined?
Define sin θ.
questions.explanation: It is a basic trigonometric ratio.

questions.question: Define tan θ.
questions.explanation: It is the ratio of perpendicular to base.

questions.question: Do trigonometric ratios depend on the size of the triangle?
questions.explanation: They depend only on the angle.

questions.question: Which ratio is commonly used to find height?
questions.explanation: tan θ relates height and distance.

notes.title: Trigonometric Ratios
notes.content: Six ratios are defined for a right-angled triangle: sin, cos, tan, cosec, sec, and cot.

notes.title: Reciprocal Ratios
notes.content: cosec, sec, and cot are reciprocals of sin, cos, and tan respectively.

notes.title: Angle Dependence
notes.content: Trigonometric ratios depend only on the angle and not on the size of the triangle.

facts.fact: Trigonometric ratios are defined only for right-angled triangles.
Standard trigonometric values are commonly used in calculations.
